title: ShadowRealm
description: ShadowRealm is my personal homelab: a self-hosted infrastructure stack running on Proxmox VE, covering media, network services, monitoring, secrets management, and a private container registry. Documented here as a reference for the architectural decisions, not as a public project.
story: Built because I wanted to actually own my infrastructure, not rent it. Every service that runs in ShadowRealm is something I chose to run myself instead of depending on a third-party SaaS.
journey: ShadowRealm started as a single Raspberry Pi running Pi-hole and gradually expanded into a proper homelab with Proxmox, LXC containers, and a structured network. The jump from "stuff running on a Pi" to "actual infrastructure" happened when I started caring about uptime, backup strategy, and secrets management. Documenting it forces the architecture to be real — if it can't be explained, it's not designed.
forWho: Self-hosters, homelab enthusiasts, and anyone who wants to see how a solo developer structures private infrastructure.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Proxmox VE hypervisor running LXC containers and VMs. Nginx reverse proxy with Cloudflare tunnel for external access. Vault for secrets. Prometheus + Grafana for monitoring.
keyPatterns: Proxmox LXC containerization for service isolation
Cloudflare tunnel for secure external access without port forwarding
Vault for secrets and credential management
